  r.store = store\n250\t    return r\n251\t\n252\t\n253\tdef _make_router_with_response(router, response_text):\n254\t    \"\"\"Wire a mock ZAI client onto the router that returns router\n259\t\n260\t\n261\t# --- 1. classification parsing ---------------------------------------------\n262\t\n263\tclass TestIntentClassification:\n264\t    \"\"\"Each pre-canned utterance @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n399\t    def test_map_intent_type_resolves_correctly(self, intent_type):\n400\t        \"\"\"_map_intent_type maps router A divergence\n410\t        means an intent fetches the wrong — or no — sources.\n411\t        \"\"\"\n412\t        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n413\t        specs = get_fetch_commands(mapped)\n414\t        actual_sources = {spec.source for spec in specs}\n415\t\n416\t        assert actual_sources == EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n417\t            f\"Source set for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n418\t        )\n419\t\n420\t    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES))\n421\t    def test_required_sources_match_matrix(self, intent_type):\n422\t        \"\"\"Required sources for each intent match the matrix's required=True set.\"\"\"\n423\t        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n424\t        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n425\t\n426\t        assert required == EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n427\t            f\"Required sources for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n428\t        )\n429\t\n430\t    def test_expected_sets_are_consistent_with_matrix_definition(self):\n431\t        \"\"\"\n432\t        Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting\n433\t        apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have\n434\t        an expected entry, and vice-versa.\n435\t\n436\t        Compared on .value strings because FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX is keyed by the\n437\t        fetch IntentType while EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES is keyed by the router\n438\t        IntentType — sibling enums that share value strings (the same contract\n439\t        _map_intent_type relies on) but are distinct classes.\n440\t        \"\"\"\n441\t        matrix_values = {t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX}\n442\t        expected_values = {t.value for t in EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES}\n443\t\n444\t        # Every *other* matrix intent must be covered.\n447\t        assert expected_values == matrix_values, (\n448\t            \"EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES does not cover exactly the fetch matrix\"\n449\t        )\n450\t\n451\t    def test_action_intent_has_required_sources_others_optional(self):\n452\t        \"\"\"\n453\t        ACTION is the canonical 'required source' intent: its required sources\n454\t        are a proper non-empty subset of its full source set, and the\n455\t        orchestrator will treat their failure as blocking.\n456\t        \"\"\"\n457\t        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(IntentType.ACTION)\n458\t        full = {s.source for s in get_fetch_commands(mapped)}\n459\t        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n460\t\n461\t        assert required  # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n261\t        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n262\t        if cached_result is not None:\n263\t            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n264\t            empty_breakdown = {\n265\t                \"cached\": True,\n266\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n267\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n268\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n269\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n270\t                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n271\t                \"process_ms\": 0,\n272\t                \"total_ms\": 0,\n273\t                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n274\t            }\n275\t            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n276\t\n277\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n278\t        retry_count = 0\n279\t        raw_response = None\n280\t\n281\t        try:\n282\t            # Measure total classification time\n283\t            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n284\t\n285\t            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n286\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n287\t\n288\t            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n289\t            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n290\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n291\t\n292\t            # Build user message\n293\t            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n294\t\n295\t            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n296\t\n297\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n298\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n299\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n300\t\n301\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n302\t\n303\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n304\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n305\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n306\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n307\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n308\t                user_message=user_message,\n309\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n310\t                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\n311\t                temperature=0.0,\n312\t                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n313\t            )\n314\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n315\t\n316\t            # Extract content and timing from response\n317\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n318\t            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n319\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n320\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n321\t\n322\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n323\t            raw_response = response\n324\t\n325\t            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n326\t            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n327\t            calculated_inference_ms = None\n328\t            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n329\t                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n330\t\n331\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n332\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n333\t            try:\n334\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n335\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n336\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n337\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n338\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n339\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n340\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n341\t                raise e\n342\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n343\t\n344\t            # Measure classification processing time\n345\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n346\t            classifications = []\n347\t\n348\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n349\t                # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n372\t            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n373\t            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n374\t            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n375\t            logger.info(\n376\t                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n377\t                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n378\t                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n379\t                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n380\t                f\"proxy_inference_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n381\t                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n382\t                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n383\t                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n384\t                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n385\t            )\n386\t\n387\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n388\t\n389\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n390\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n391\t            timing_breakdown = {\n392\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n393\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n394\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n395\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n396\t                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n397\t                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n398\t                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n399\t                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n400\t            }\n401\t\n402\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n403\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n404\t\n405\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n406\t\n407\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n408\t            \"\"\"\n409\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n410\t\n411\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n412\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n413\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n414\t            \"\"\"\n415\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n416\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n417\t\n418\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n419\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n420\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n421\t                retry_count += 1\n422\t                try:\n423\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n424\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n425\t                        utterance=utterance,\n426\t                        session_id=session_id,\n427\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n428\t                    )\n429\t                    return classifications, _\n430\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n431\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n432\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n433\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n434\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n435\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n436\t                    ) from retry_e\n437\t\n438\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n439\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n440\t                parse_error=str(e),\n441\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n442\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n443\t            ) from e\n444\t\n445\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n446\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n447\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n448\t\n449\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n450\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n451\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n452\t\n453\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n454\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n455\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n456\t\n457\t        except Exception as e:\n458\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n459\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n460\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n461\t\n462\t    async def route_utterance(\n463\t        self,\n464\t        utterance: str,\n465\t        utterance_id: str,\n466\t        session_id: str,\n467\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n468\t        \"\"\"\n469\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n470\t\n471\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n472\t\n473\t        Args:\n474\t            utterance: The user utterance\n475\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n476\t            session_id: Session ID\n477\t\n478\t        Returns:\n479\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n480\t\n481\t        Raises:\n482\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n483\t        \"\"\"\n484\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n485\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n486\t\n487\t        try:\n488\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n489\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n490\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n491\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n492\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n493\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n494\t\n495\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n496\t            try:\n497\t                store = await self._get_store()\n498\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n499\t                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n500\t            except Exception as e:\n501\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n502\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n503\t\n504\t            routed_intents = []\n505\t            for classification in classifications:\n506\t                # Create intent ID\n507\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n508\t\n509\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n510\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n511\t                    classification=classification,\n512\t                    session_id=session_id,\n513\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n514\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n515\t                )\n516\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n517\t\n518\t            return routed_intents\n519\t\n520\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n521\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n522\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n523\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n524\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n525\t                utterance=utterance,\n526\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n527\t                session_id=session_id,\n528\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n529\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n530\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n531\t            )\n532\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n533\t\n534\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n535\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n536\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n537\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n538\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n539\t                utterance=utterance,\n540\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n541\t                session_id=session_id,\n542\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n543\t            )\n544\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n545\t\n546\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n547\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n548\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n549\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n550\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n551\t                utterance=utterance,\n552\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n553\t                session_id=session_id,\n554\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n555\t            )\n556\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n557\t\n558\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n559\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n560\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n561\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n562\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n563\t                utterance=utterance,\n564\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n565\t                session_id=session_id,\n566\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n567\t            )\n568\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n569\t\n570\t    async def process_intent(\n571\t        self,\n572\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n573\t    ) -> dict:\n574\t        \"\"\"\n575\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n576\t\n577\t        Args:\n578\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n579\t\n580\t        Returns:\n581\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n582\t        \"\"\"\n583\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n584\t\n585\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n586\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n587\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n588\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n589\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n590\t        # the dispatch itself.\n591\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n592\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n593\t\n594\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n595\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n596\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n597\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n598\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n599\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n600\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n601\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n602\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n603\t            )\n604\t\n605\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n606\t            store = await self._get_store()\n607\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n608\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n609\t                \"resolved\",\n610\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n611\t            )\n612\t\n613\t            return {\n614\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n615\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n616\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n617\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n618\t            }\n619\t\n620\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n621\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n622\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n623\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n624\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n625\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n626\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n627\t            )\n628\t\n629\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n630\t            store = await self._get_store()\n631\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n632\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n633\t                \"resolved\",\n634\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n635\t            )\n636\t\n637\t            return {\n638\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n639\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n640\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n641\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n642\t            }\n643\t\n644\t        try:\n645\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n646\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n647\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n648\t            else:\n649\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n650\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n651\t        except Exception:\n652\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n653\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n654\t            raise\n655\t\n656\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n657\t        return result\n658\t\n659\t    async def _persist_timings(\n660\t        self,\n661\t        intent_id: str,\n662\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n663\t    ) -> None:\n664\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n665\t        try:\n666\t            store = await self._get_store()\n667\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n668\t        except Exception as e:\n669\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n670\t\n671\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n672\t        self,\n673\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n674\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n675\t    ) -> dict:\n676\t        \"\"\"\n677\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n678\t\n679\t        Args:\n680\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n681\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n682\t                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n683\t\n684\t        Returns:\n685\t            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n686\t        \"\"\"\n687\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n688\t\n689\t        logger.info(\n690\t            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n691\t            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n692\t        )\n693\t\n694\t        try:\n695\t            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n696\t            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n697\t                classification.intent_type,\n698\t                classification.lookup_kind\n699\t            )\n700\t\n701\t            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n702\t            from ..registry import get_project\n703\t            repo_path = None\n704\t            ssh_target = None\n705\t            host_alias = None\n706\t            registry = get_registry()\n707\t            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n708\t                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n709\t                if location = entry.display_path\n714\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n715\t                else:\n716\t                    logger.info(f\"No _fetch_argocd_app).\n722\t            project_cfg = (\n723\t                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n724\t                if classification.project_slug else None\n725\t            )\n726\t\n727\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n728\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n729\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n730\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n731\t                context=FetchContext(\n732\t                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n733\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n734\t                    repo_path=repo_path,\n735\t                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n736\t                    host_alias=host_alias,\n737\t                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n738\t                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n739\t                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n740\t                ),\n741\t            )\n742\t\n743\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n744\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n745\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n746\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n747\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n748\t            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n749\t            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n750\t\n751\t            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n752\t                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n753\t                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n754\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n755\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n756\t\n757\t                # Increment completed count\n758\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n759\t\n760\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n761\t                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n762\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n763\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n764\t                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n765\t                    total=total_sources,\n766\t                    source_name=source.value,\n767\t                    source_status=result.status,\n768\t                ))\n769\t\n770\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n771\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n772\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n773\t                timings.record(\n774\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n775\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n776\t                )\n777\t\n778\t            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n779\t            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n780\t                logger.error(\n781\t                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n782\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n783\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n784\t                )\n785\t\n786\t                # Build failed sources list for error event\n787\t                failed_sources = []\n788\t                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n789\t                    failed_sources.append({\n790\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n791\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n792\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n793\t                    })\n794\t\n795\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n796\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n797\t                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n798\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n799\t                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n800\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n801\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n802\t                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n803\t                )\n804\t\n805\t                # Update intent status to failed\n806\t                store = get_store()\n807\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n808\t                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n809\t                    \"failed\",\n810\t                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n811\t                )\n812\t\n813\t                return {\n814\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n815\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n816\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n817\t                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n818\t                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n819\t                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n820\t                }\n821\t\n822\t            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n823\t            required_sources_failed = [\n824\t                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n825\t                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n826\t                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n827\t            ]\n828\t\n829\t            if error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n838\t                )\n839\t\n840\t                return {\n841\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n842\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n843\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n844\t                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n845\t                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n846\t                }\n847\t\n848\t            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n849\t            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n850\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n851\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n852\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n853\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n854\t                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n855\t                urgency=classification.urgency,\n856\t            )\n857\t\n858\t            synth_start = timings.clock()\n859\t            try:\n860\t                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n861\t            except Exception as synth_e:\n862\t                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n863\t                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n864\t                logger.error(\n865\t                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n866\t                )\n867\t\n868\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n869\t                await session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n873\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n874\t                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n875\t                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n876\t                )\n877\t\n878\t                # Update intent status to failed\n879\t                store = get_store()\n880\t                await )\n885\t\n886\t                return {\n887\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n888\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n889\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n890\t                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n891\t                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n892\t                    \"degraded\": True,\n893\t                }\n894\t\n895\t            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n896\t            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n901\t            store = get_store()\n902\t            _topic_type_map = {\n903\t                IntentType.ACTION:    topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n908\t                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n909\t                topic_type=topic_type,\n910\t                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n911\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n912\t            )\n913\t            await lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n920\t            )\n921\t\n922\t            result_id = await store.create_result(\n923\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n924\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n925\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n926\t                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n927\t                data=synthesize_result.data,\n928\t                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n929\t                result_type=result_type,\n930\t            )\n931\t\n932\t            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no             result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n938\t                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n939\t                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n940\t            )\n941\t\n942\t            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n943\t            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n952\t                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n953\t                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n954\t                \"coverage\": \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n958\t                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n959\t                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n960\t            }\n961\t\n962\t        except Exception as e:\n963\t            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n964\t\n965\t            # Detect terminal failure_reason=str(e),\n969\t                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n970\t            )\n971\t\n972\t            return {\n973\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n974\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n975\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n976\t                \"error\": str(e),\n977\t                \"message\": {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_1c92846e7da9496fa8ca453c","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tZAI Response Parser - Centralized utility for parsing LLM responses.\n3\t\n4\tHandles:\n5\t1. ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n6\t2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n7\t3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n8\t4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n9\t\n10\tThis utility is used across:\n11\t- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n12\t- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n13\t- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n14\t- Any other LLM interaction points\n15\t\n16\t## Error Handling Patterns\n17\t\n18\tWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n19\t\n20\t### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\n21\tUse when:\n22\t- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n23\t- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n24\t- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n25\t\n26\tExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n27\t\n28\tSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n29\t\n30\t### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\n31\tUse when:\n32\t- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n33\t- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n34\t- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n35\t\n36\tExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n37\t\n38\tSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n39\t\n40\t### Documentation\n41\tSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n42\t\"\"\"\n43\t\n44\timport json\n45\tfrom logging import getLogger\n46\tfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Union, cast\n47\t\n48\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n49\t\n50\t\n51\tclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n52\t    \"\"\"\n53\t    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n54\t\n55\t    Attributes:\n56\t        message: Human-readable error message\n57\t        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n58\t\n59\t    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n60\t    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n61\t    response snippets in error events and logs.\n62\t    \"\"\"\n63\t\n64\t    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n65\t        self.raw_response = raw_response\n66\t        super().__init__(message)\n67\t\n68\t\n69\tdef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n70\t    \"\"\"\n71\t    Strip markdown code fences from a response using optimized position-based parsing.\n72\t\n73\t    Handles:\n74\t    - ```json ... ```\n75\t    - ``` ... ```\n76\t    - Content with ``` in the middle (rfind finds closing fence correctly)\n77\t    - Incomplete fences (no closing fence - strips opening only)\n78\t    - Bare JSON (no fences)\n79\t\n80\t    Performance: Uses find()/rfind() for position-based fence removal, avoiding intermediate\n81\t    string allocations from split()/rsplit(). The rfind() approach correctly handles content\n82\t    with embedded ``` by finding the LAST ``` (closing fence), not the second occurrence.\n83\t\n84\t    Args:\n85\t        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n86\t\n87\t    Returns:\n88\t        Text with markdown fences removed\n89\t\n90\t    Examples:\n91\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n92\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n93\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n94\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n95\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n96\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n97\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"code\": \"```hello```\"}\\\\n```')\n98\t        '{\"code\": \"```hello```\"}'\n99\t    \"\"\"\n100\t    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n101\t    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n102\t        return raw\n103\t\n104\t    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n105\t    text = raw.strip()\n106\t\n107\t    # Fast manual fence stripping using position-based search\n108\t    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n109\t    # Uses rfind() to find LAST ``` (closing fence), which handles content with embedded ```\n110\t    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n111\t        # Find first newline after opening fence\n112\t        first_newline = text.find(\"\\n\")\n113\t\n114\t        # Find closing fence from END (last ``` in the text)\n115\t        # This correctly handles content with ``` embedded in it\n116\t        fence_end = text.rfind(\"```\")\n117\t\n118\t        if fence_end != -1 and fence_end > 3:  # Must be after opening fence\n119\t            # Complete fence: extract content between opening and closing\n120\t            # Content is after first newline, before closing fence\n121\t            if first_newline != -1 and fence_end > first_newline:\n122\t                text = text[first_newline + 1:fence_end].strip()\n123\t            else:\n124\t                # No newline after opening fence or fence before newline (malformed)\n125\t                text = \"\"\n126\t        else:\n127\t            # Incomplete fence: only opening fence present\n128\t            # Strip opening fence and return the rest\n129\t            if first_newline != -1:\n130\t                text = text[first_newline + 1:].strip()\n131\t            else:\n132\t                # Opening fence with no content\n133\t                text = \"\"\n134\t\n135\t    return text\n136\t\n137\t\n138\tdef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n139\t    \"\"\"\n140\t    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n141\t\n142\t    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n143\t    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n144\t\n145\t    Args:\n146\t        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n147\t\n148\t    Returns:\n149\t        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n150\t\n151\t    Examples:\n152\t        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n153\t        {\"content\": [...]}\n154\t        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n155\t        {\"content\": [...]}\n156\t    \"\"\"\n157\t    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n158\t    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n159\t        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n160\t        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n161\t        return inner\n162\t\n163\t    return response_data\n164\t\n165\t\n166\tdef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n167\t    \"\"\"\n168\t    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n169\t\n170\t    Args:\n171\t        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n172\t\n173\t    Returns:\n174\t        Extracted text content\n175\t\n176\t    Raises:\n177\t        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n178\t    \"\"\"\n179\t    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n180\t\n181\t    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n182\t        text = cast(str, content[0].get(\"text\", \"\"))\n183\t        if text:\n184\t            return text\n185\t\n186\t    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n187\t    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n188\t    return str(content)\n189\t\n190\t\n191\tdef parse_llm_response(\n192\t    raw_text: str,\n193\t    *,\n194\t    strip_fences: bool = True,\n195\t    expect_json: bool = True,\n196\t) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n197\t    \"\"\"\n198\t    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n199\t\n200\t    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n201\t    It handles the common pattern of:\n202\t    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n203\t    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n204\t    3. Providing clear error messages\n205\t\n206\t    Performance: Optimized with fast fence stripping and minimal overhead.\n207\t    Uses strip_markdown_fences() for fence removal which is 7-179x faster than regex.\n208\t\n209\t    Args:\n210\t        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n211\t        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n212\t        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n213\t\n214\t    Returns:\n215\t        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n216\t\n217\t    Raises:\n218\t        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n219\t\n220\t    Examples:\n221\t        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n222\t        {'a': 1}\n223\t        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n224\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n225\t    \"\"\"\n226\t    try:\n227\t        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present (reuses optimized function)\n228\t        if strip_fences:\n229\t            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n230\t        else:\n231\t            text = raw_text.strip() if raw_text else \"\"\n232\t\n233\t        # Step 2: Check for empty input (after fence stripping)\n234\t        # Use strip() to catch whitespace-only strings\n235\t        if not text or not text.strip():\n236\t            if expect_json:\n237\t                raise ParseLLMError(\n238\t                    \"Empty response provided\",\n239\t                    raw_response=raw_text,\n240\t                )\n241\t            return raw_text\n242\t\n243\t        # Step 3: Parse JSON if expected\n244\t        if expect_json:\n245\t            try:\n246\t                return json.loads(text)\n247\t            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n248\t                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n249\t                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n250\t                raise ParseLLMError(\n251\t                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n252\t                    raw_response=raw_text,\n253\t                ) from e\n254\t\n255\t        return text\n256\t\n257\t    except ParseLLMError:\n258\t        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n259\t        raise\n260\t    except Exception as e:\n261\t        raise ParseLLMError(\n262\t            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n263\t            raw_response=raw_text,\n264\t        ) from e\n265\t\n266\t\n267\tdef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n268\t    response_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n269\t    *,\n270\t    extract_text: bool = True,\n271\t    strip_fences: bool = True,\n272\t    expect_json: bool = True,\n273\t) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n274\t    \"\"\"\n275\t    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n276\t\n277\t    This handles the full flow:\n278\t    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n279\t    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n280\t    3. Strip markdown fences\n281\t    4. Parse JSON if expected\n282\t\n283\t    Args:\n284\t        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n285\t        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n286\t        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n287\t        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n288\t\n289\t    Returns:\n290\t        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n291\t\n292\t    Raises:\n293\t        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n294\t\n295\t    Examples:\n296\t        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n297\t        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n298\t        {'a': 1}\n299\t    \"\"\"\n300\t    try:\n301\t        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n302\t        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n303\t\n304\t        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n305\t        if extract_text:\n306\t            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n307\t        else:\n308\t            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n309\t            return unwrapped\n310\t\n311\t        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n312\t        return parse_llm_response(\n313\t            text,\n314\t            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n315\t            expect_json=expect_json,\n316\t        )\n317\t\n318\t    except ParseLLMError:\n319\t        raise\n320\t    except Exception as e:\n321\t        raise ParseLLMError(\n322\t            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n323\t            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n324\t        ) from e\n325\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_264820303f6f4c3385466953","session_id":"47f31957-5b2b-4936-9121-7a084006ed46","uuid":"b1d18379-98fe-475f-8609-1baa902e1326","timestamp":"2026-07-24T06:31:35.071Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore intent router implementation"}

Segment the utterance into \\\"\\n124\\t    \\\"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \\\"\\n125\\t    \\\"intent objects.\\\"\\n126\\t)\\n127\\t\\n128\\t\\n129\\tclass IntentRouter:\\n130\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n131\\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\\n132\\t\\n133\\t    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\\n134\\t    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\\n135\\t\\n136\\t    Latency optimizations:\\n137\\t    - LRU cache for repeated utterances (5-minute TTL)\\n138\\t    - Reduced max_tokens (128 vs 256) for faster generation\\n139\\t    - Dedicated 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\\n140\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n141\\t\\n142\\t    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\\n143\\t        self.store = store\\n144\\t        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\\n145\\t        self._zai_client = None\\n146\\t        self._router_zai_client = None  # Router needs fail-fast behavior with aggressive timeout\\n160\\t            # 8-second timeout allows: ~2s connection + ~4-5s inference + ~1s parsing\\n161\\t            self._router_zai_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\\n162\\t        return self._router_zai_client\\n163\\t\\n164\\t    def _get_utterance_hash(self, utterance: str) -> str:\\n165\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Generate hash key for utterance caching.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n166\\t        import hashlib\\n167\\t        return hashlib.md5(utterance.encode()).hexdigest()\\n168\\t\\n169\\t    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\\n170\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Check cache for existing classification.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n171\\t        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\\n172\\t        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\\n173\\t\\n174\\t        if cache_key in _ROUTER_CACHE:\\n175\\t            classifications, timestamp = _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\\n176\\t            age = time.time() - timestamp\\n177\\t            if age < _CACHE_TTL:\\n178\\t                logger.info(f\\\"Router cache HIT for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\\\")\\n179\\t                return classifications\\n180\\t            else:\\n181\\t                # Remove expired entry\\n182\\t                del _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\\n183\\t                logger.info(f\\\"Router cache EXPIRED for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]}\\\")\\n184\\t\\n185\\t        return None\\n186\\t\\n187\\t    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\\n188\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Cache classification result.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n189\\t        # Prune cache if at capacity\\n190\\t        if len(_ROUTER_CACHE) >= _MAX_CACHE_SIZE:\\n191\\t            # Remove oldest entry (first in dict)\\n192\\t            oldest_key = next(iter(_ROUTER_CACHE))\\n193\\t            del _ROUTER_CACHE[oldest_key]\\n194\\t            logger.info(f\\\"Router cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\\\")\\n195\\t\\n196\\t        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\\n197\\t        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\\n198\\t        _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key] = (classifications, time.time())\\n199\\t        logger.info(f\\\"Router cache STORED utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (cache size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\\\")\\n200\\t\\n201\\t    async def _get_store(self):\\n202\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Get or create session store.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n203\\t        if self.store is None:\\n204\\t            self.store = get_store()\\n205\\t        return self.store\\n206\\t\\n207\\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\\n208\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n209\\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\\n210\\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\\n211\\t        return self._reload_manager\\n212\\t\\n213\\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\\n214\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n215\\t        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\\n216\\t\\n217\\t        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\\n218\\t        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\\n219\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n220\\t        try:\\n221\\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\\\"router\\\")\\n222\\t        except Exception as e:\\n223\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\\\")\\n224\\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\\n225\\t\\n226\\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\\n227\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\\n228\\t\\n229\\t        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\\n230\\t        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\\n231\\t        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\\n232\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n233\\t        return self._load_router_prompt()\\n234\\t\\n235\\t    async def classify_utterance(\\n236\\t        self,\\n237\\t        utterance: str,\\n238\\t        session_id: str,\\n239\\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\\n240\\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\\n241\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n242\\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\\n243\\t\\n244\\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\\n245\\t\\n246\\t        Args:\\n247\\t            utterance: The user utterance\\n248\\t            session_id: Session ID for context\\n249\\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\\n250\\t\\n251\\t        Returns:\\n252\\t            List of IntentClassification objects\\n253\\t\\n254\\t        Raises:\\n255\\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\\n256\\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\\n257\\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\\n258\\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\\n259\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n260\\t        # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\\n261\\t        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\\n262\\t        if cached_result is not None:\\n263\\t            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\\n264\\t            empty_breakdown = {\\n265\\t                \\\"cached\\\": True,\\n266\\t                \\\"prompt_construction_ms\\\": 0,\\n267\\t                \\\"proxy_call_ms\\\": 0,\\n268\\t                \\\"proxy_network_ms\\\": 0,\\n269\\t                \\\"proxy_inference_ms\\\": 0,\\n270\\t                \\\"json_parse_ms\\\": 0,\\n271\\t                \\\"process_ms\\\": 0,\\n272\\t                \\\"total_ms\\\": 0,\\n273\\t                \\\"intents_count\\\": len(cached_result),\\n274\\t            }\\n275\\t            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\\n276\\t\\n277\\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\\n278\\t        retry_count = 0\\n279\\t        raw_response = None\\n280\\t\\n281\\t        try:\\n282\\t            # Measure total classification time\\n283\\t            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\\n284\\t\\n285\\t            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\\n286\\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\\n287\\t\\n288\\t            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\\n289\\t            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\\n290\\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\\n291\\t\\n292\\t            # Build user message\\n293\\t            user_message = f\\\"Classify this utterance:\\\\n\\\\n{utterance}\\\"\\n294\\t\\n295\\t            logger.info(f\\\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\\\")\\n296\\t\\n297\\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\\n298\\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\\n299\\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\\n300\\t\\n301\\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\\n302\\t\\n303\\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\\n304\\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\\n305\\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\\n306\\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\\n307\\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\\n308\\t                user_message=user_message,\\n309\\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\\n310\\t                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\\n311\\t                temperature=0.0,\\n312\\t                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\\n313\\t            )\\n314\\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\\n315\\t\\n316\\t            # Extract content and timing from response\\n317\\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\\n318\\t            response = response_data.get(\\\"content\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n319\\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\\\"timing_network_ms\\\")\\n320\\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\\\"timing_inference_ms\\\")\\n321\\t\\n322\\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\\n323\\t            raw_response = response\\n324\\t\\n325\\t            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\\n326\\t            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\\n327\\t            calculated_inference_ms = None\\n328\\t            if timing_network_ms is not None:\\n329\\t                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\\n330\\t\\n331\\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\\n332\\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\\n333\\t            try:\\n334\\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\\n335\\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\\n336\\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\\n337\\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\\n338\\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\\n339\\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\\n340\\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\\n341\\t                raise e\\n342\\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\\n343\\t\\n344\\t            # Measure classification processing time\\n345\\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\\n346\\t            classifications = []\\n347\\t\\n348\\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\\n349\\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\\n350\\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\\\"intent_type\\\", \\\"status\\\")\\n351\\t                try:\\n352\\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\\n353\\t                except ValueError:\\n354\\t                    logger.warning(f\\\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\\\")\\n355\\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\\n356\\t\\n357\\t                classification = IntentClassification(\\n358\\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\\n359\\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\\\"project_slug\\\"),\\n360\\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\\\"confidence\\\", 0.8)),\\n361\\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\\\"utterance_fragment\\\", utterance),\\n362\\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\\\"reasoning\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n363\\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\\\"urgency\\\", \\\"normal\\\"),\\n364\\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\\\"lookup_kind\\\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\\n365\\t                )\\n366\\t                classifications.append(classification)\\n367\\t            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\\n368\\t\\n369\\t            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\\n370\\t\\n371\\t            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\\n372\\t            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\\n373\\t            network_str = f\\\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\\\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \\\"N/A\\\"\\n374\\t            calculated_inference_str = f\\\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\\\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \\\"N/A\\\"\\n375\\t            logger.info(\\n376\\t                f\\\"router_timing breakdown: \\\"\\n377\\t                f\\\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \\\"\\n378\\t                f\\\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \\\"\\n379\\t                f\\\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \\\"\\n380\\t                f\\\"proxy_inference_ms={calculated_inference_str} \\\"\\n381\\t                f\\\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \\\"\\n382\\t                f\\\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \\\"\\n383\\t                f\\\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \\\"\\n384\\t                f\\\"intents={len(classifications)}\\\"\\n385\\t            )\\n386\\t\\n387\\t            logger.info(f\\\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\\\")\\n388\\t\\n389\\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\\n390\\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\\n391\\t            timing_breakdown = {\\n392\\t                \\\"prompt_construction_ms\\\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\\n393\\t                \\\"proxy_call_ms\\\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\\n394\\t                \\\"proxy_network_ms\\\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\\n395\\t                \\\"proxy_inference_ms\\\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\\n396\\t                \\\"json_parse_ms\\\": round(parse_ms, 2),\\n397\\t                \\\"process_ms\\\": round(process_ms, 2),\\n398\\t                \\\"total_ms\\\": round(total_ms, 2),\\n399\\t                \\\"intents_count\\\": len(classifications),\\n400\\t            }\\n401\\t\\n402\\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\\n403\\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\\n404\\t\\n405\\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\\n406\\t\\n407\\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\\n408\\t            \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n409\\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\\n410\\t\\n411\\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\\n412\\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\\n413\\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\\n414\\t            \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n415\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\\\")\\n416\\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\\n417\\t\\n418\\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\\n419\\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\\n420\\t                logger.info(\\\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\\\")\\n421\\t                retry_count += 1\\n422\\t                try:\\n423\\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\\n424\\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\\n425\\t                        utterance=utterance,\\n426\\t                        session_id=session_id,\\n427\\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\\n428\\t                    )\\n429\\t                    return classifications, _\\n430\\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\\n431\\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\\n432\\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\\n433\\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\\n434\\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\\n435\\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\\n436\\t                    ) from retry_e\\n437\\t\\n438\\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\\n439\\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\\n440\\t                parse_error=str(e),\\n441\\t                raw_output=raw_output,\\n442\\t                retry_count=retry_count,\\n443\\t            ) from e\\n444\\t\\n445\\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\\n446\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\\\")\\n447\\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\\\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\\\") from e\\n448\\t\\n449\\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\\n450\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\\\")\\n451\\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\\\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\\\") from e\\n452\\t\\n453\\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\\n454\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\\\")\\n455\\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\\\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\\\") from e\\n456\\t\\n457\\t        except Exception as e:\\n458\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\\\")\\n459\\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\\n460\\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\\\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\\\") from e\\n461\\t\\n462\\t    async def route_utterance(\\n463\\t        self,\\n464\\t        utterance: str,\\n465\\t        utterance_id: str,\\n466\\t        session_id: str,\\n467\\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\\n468\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n469\\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\\n470\\t\\n471\\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\\n472\\t\\n473\\t        Args:\\n474\\t            utterance: The user utterance\\n475\\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\\n476\\t            session_id: Session ID\\n477\\t\\n478\\t        Returns:\\n479\\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\\n480\\t\\n481\\t        Raises:\\n482\\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\\n483\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n484\\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\\n485\\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\\n486\\t\\n487\\t        try:\\n488\\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\\n489\\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\\n490\\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\\n491\\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\\n492\\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\\n493\\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\\n494\\t\\n495\\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\\n496\\t            try:\\n497\\t                store = await self._get_store()\\n498\\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\\n499\\t                logger.info(f\\\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\\\")\\n500\\t            except Exception as e:\\n501\\t                logger.warning(f\\\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\\\")\\n502\\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\\n503\\t\\n504\\t            routed_intents = []\\n505\\t            for classification in classifications:\\n506\\t                # Create intent ID\\n507\\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\\n508\\t\\n509\\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\\n510\\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\\n511\\t                    classification=classification,\\n512\\t                    session_id=session_id,\\n513\\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\\n514\\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\\n515\\t                )\\n516\\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\\n517\\t\\n518\\t            return routed_intents\\n519\\t\\n520\\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\\n521\\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\\n522\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\\\")\\n523\\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\\n524\\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\\n525\\t                utterance=utterance,\\n526\\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\\n527\\t                session_id=session_id,\\n528\\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\\n529\\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\\n530\\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\\n531\\t            )\\n532\\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\\n533\\t\\n534\\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\\n535\\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\\n536\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Router timeout: {e}\\\")\\n537\\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\\n538\\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\\n539\\t                utterance=utterance,\\n540\\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\\n541\\t                session_id=session_id,\\n542\\t                error_reason=\\\"timeout\\\",\\n543\\t            )\\n544\\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\\n545\\t\\n546\\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\\n547\\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\\n548\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\\\")\\n549\\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\\n550\\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\\n551\\t                utterance=utterance,\\n552\\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\\n553\\t                session_id=session_id,\\n554\\t                error_reason=\\\"quota_exhausted\\\",\\n555\\t            )\\n556\\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\\n557\\t\\n558\\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\\n559\\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\\n560\\t            logger.error(f\\\"Router proxy error: {e}\\\")\\n561\\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\\n562\\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\\n563\\t                utterance=utterance,\\n564\\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\\n565\\t                session_id=session_id,\\n566\\t                error_reason=\\\"proxy_down\\\",\\n567\\t            )\\n568\\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\\n569\\t\\n570\\t    async def process_intent(\\n571\\t        self,\\n572\\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\\n573\\t    ) -> dict:\\n574\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n575\\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\\n576\\t\\n577\\t        Args:\\n578\\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\\n579\\t\\n580\\t        Returns:\\n581\\t            Result dictionary with status and data\\n582\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n583\\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\\n584\\t\\n585\\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\\n586\\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\\n587\\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\\n588\\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\\n589\\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\\n590\\t        # the dispatch itself.\\n591\\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\\n592\\t        timings.record(\\\"router_ms\\\", routed_intent.router_ms)\\n593\\t\\n594\\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\\n595\\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\\n596\\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\\n597\\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\\n598\\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\\n599\\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\\n600\\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\\n601\\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\\n602\\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\\n603\\t            )\\n604\\t\\n605\\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\\n606\\t            store = await self._get_store()\\n607\\t            await store.update_intent_status(\\n608\\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\\n609\\t                \\\"resolved\\\",\\n610\\t                \\\"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\\\",\\n611\\t            )\\n612\\t\\n613\\t            return {\\n614\\t                \\\"intent_id\\\": routed_intent.intent_id,\\n615\\t                \\\"intent_type\\\": classification.intent_type.value,\\n616\\t                \\\"status\\\": \\\"design_only\\\",\\n617\\t                \\\"message\\\": \\\"Action execution is not yet available\\\",\\n618\\t            }\\n619\\t\\n620\\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\\n621\\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\\n622\\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\\n623\\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\\n624\\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\\n625\\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\\n626\\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\\n627\\t            )\\n628\\t\\n629\\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\\n630\\t            store = await self._get_store()\\n631\\t            await store.update_intent_status(\\n632\\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\\n633\\t                \\\"resolved\\\",\\n634\\t                \\\"Reminders are not available yet\\\",\\n635\\t            )\\n636\\t\\n637\\t            return {\\n638\\t                \\\"intent_id\\\": routed_intent.intent_id,\\n639\\t                \\\"intent_type\\\": classification.intent_type.value,\\n640\\t                \\\"status\\\": \\\"unavailable\\\",\\n641\\t                \\\"message\\\": \\\"Reminders are not available yet\\\",\\n642\\t            }\\n643\\t\\n644\\t        try:\\n645\\t            # output tokens\\n212\\t            base_network_latency = 100  # Base VPN round-trip latency\\n213\\t            if output_tokens > 0:\\n214\\t                # Transfer time: ~0.3ms per token on
